meaning: n.一时的兴致,幻想,反复无常,怪念头,奇想
examples: He was forced to pander to her every whim.
We decided, more or less on a whim, to sail to Morocco.
A still picture could only imply the existence of time, while time in a novel passed at the whim of the reader.
Then deliver those superbly rather than catering to their every whim or mild interest.
Whim worshipping only leads to self destruction, and any one is free to destroy themselves.
